happy.content: Happiness is not something ready-made. It comes from your own actions.
happy.author: Dalai Lama

happy.content: The secret of happiness is not in doing what one likes, but in liking what one does.
happy.author: James M. Barrie

happy.content: Joy is not in things; it is in us.
happy.author: Richard Wagner

happy.content: For every minute you are angry you lose sixty seconds of happiness.
happy.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

sad.content: The cure for anything is salt water: sweat, tears or the sea.
sad.author: Isak Dinesen

sad.content: The soul would have no rainbow had the eyes no tears.
sad.author: John Vance Cheney

sad.content: Heavy hearts, like heavy clouds in the sky, are best relieved by the letting of a little water.
sad.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

sad.content: Sadness flies away on the wings of time.
sad.author: Jean de La Fontaine

anxious.content: You don't have to control your thoughts. You just have to stop letting them control you.
anxious.author: Dan Millman

anxious.content: Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom.
anxious.author: Søren Kierkegaard

anxious.content: Do not anticipate trouble, or worry about what may never happen. Keep in the sunlight.
anxious.author: Benjamin Franklin

calm.content: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
calm.author: Buddha

calm.content: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
calm.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

calm.content: Quiet the mind and the soul will speak.
calm.author: Ma Jaya Sati Bhagavati

calm.content: Calmness is the cradle of power.
calm.author: Josiah Gilbert Holland

motivated.content: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
motivated.author: Steve Jobs

motivated.content: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
motivated.author: Theodore Roosevelt

motivated.content: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
motivated.author: Winston Churchill

motivated.content: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
motivated.author: Mark Twain

angry.content: Anger is an acid that can do more harm to the vessel in which it is stored than to anything on which it is poured.
angry.author: Mark Twain

angry.content: For every minute you remain angry, you give up sixty seconds of peace of mind.
angry.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

angry.content: The best fighter is never angry.
angry.author: Lao Tzu

general.content: Every emotion has its place in the human experience.
general.author: Unknown

general.content: Every day is a new beginning. Take a deep breath and start again.
general.author: Unknown

general.content: Self-awareness is the key to self-mastery.
general.author: Unknown
